title: Go Input
content:
### Reading User Input in Go
The `fmt` package provides functions for reading input.

### fmt.Scan

```go
var name string
fmt.Print("Enter your name: ")
fmt.Scan(&name)
fmt.Println("Hello,", name)
```

### fmt.Scanf

Formatted input:

```go
var name string
var age int
fmt.Print("Enter name and age: ")
fmt.Scanf("%s %d", &name, &age)
```

### fmt.Scanln

Reads until newline:

```go
var name string
fmt.Print("Enter name: ")
fmt.Scanln(&name)
```

### bufio.Scanner for Lines

```go
import (
    "bufio"
    "os"
)

scanner := bufio.NewScanner(os.Stdin)
fmt.Print("Enter a line: ")
scanner.Scan()
input := scanner.Text()
fmt.Println("You entered:", input)
```

### Reading Multiple Lines

```go
scanner := bufio.NewScanner(os.Stdin)
fmt.Println("Enter lines (Ctrl+D to stop):")
for scanner.Scan() {
    line := scanner.Text()
    fmt.Println("Got:", line)
}
```

### Reading Integers

```go
var num int
fmt.Print("Enter a number: ")
_, err := fmt.Scan(&num)
if err != nil {
    fmt.Println("Invalid input:", err)
}
```

| Method | Best For |
|--------|----------|
| `fmt.Scan` | Space-separated values |
| `fmt.Scanf` | Formatted input |
| `fmt.Scanln` | Single line |
| `bufio.Scanner` | Multi-line or complex input |

> **Key Point:** Always check the error returned by Scan functions. Invalid input can cause unexpected behavior.

quiz.question: Which function is best for reading a full line of text?
What package provides Scanner for input?
